what_it_does: Defines a single generic tldraw shape type for a visual graph canvas, renders node cards with status-driven styling and per-port nubs, implements pointer-based output-to-input connection gestures, and provides loadGraph/refresh utilities to synchronize tldraw shapes with a backend graph while drawing connecting edges as an SVG overlay.
observations: NodeShape is a TLBaseShape whose props carry node identity, type, kind, status, output, address, ref, layer, and error.
portTop is exported and shared between the card nubs and the Edges overlay so wires and nubs align vertically.
The component reads port schemas from getOINFO and node-state definitions from getNODE_STATES, deriving status words, dot colors, and dot shapes from registry render tokens.
Failed nodes surface the error string; stuck, live, and empty statuses each get distinct card body text.
Output nubs start a drag via setPointerCapture and resolve the drop target with document.elementFromPoint on the source element's pointerup.

loadGraph uses backend-provided n.position as the single source of layout truth, creates missing shapes, updates existing ones, prunes orphaned shapes, and zooms to fit only when the page started empty.
refresh updates only existing shapes with status, output, error, layer, and optional position changes.
Edges is a reactive screen-space overlay that recomputes source/target screen coordinates on camera or zoom changes and draws cubic-bezier paths anchored to exact ports.
standouts: Registry-driven status rendering: color, label, and dot shape come from served state definitions rather than hardcoded UI logic.
Pointer-capture connection gesture: output nubs capture the pointer and use elementFromPoint to find the input nub under the cursor at drop.
Backend-owned layout with orphan pruning: loadGraph treats backend node positions as authoritative and removes shapes whose node no longer exists.
conventions: Follows tldraw's ShapeUtil subclass pattern with static type/props and a React component.
Uses useEditor and useValue for editor state and reactive camera/zoom subscriptions.
Uses stopEventPropagation to keep pointer events on nubs from being interpreted as canvas interactions.
Keeps programmatic shape CRUD separate from user-driven writes to avoid feedback loops.
Relies on external module stores (registryStore) for node schemas and state definitions.
concerns: Several values are typed as any, including connection state, graph nodes, API state, and SVG line arrays.
getNODE_STATES is read directly in render instead of through a tldraw signal, so the comment notes it only updates when a props change triggers re-render.
refresh does not create or prune shapes, so a loadGraph must be used when nodes are added or removed.
The connection drop target depends on document.elementFromPoint, which can be brittle if other elements intercept pointer events.
Status label logic and body rendering logic partially duplicate the same status branches.
notes: This file is the canvas rendering layer: it turns backend graph state and registry metadata into tldraw shapes and wires, while letting the backend own layout and connections.
questions: What exact schema does api.graph return for nodes and edges?
How and when are getOINFO and getNODE_STATES populated and invalidated?
What CSS classes and variables are expected for status tokens and ring/dot shapes?
What does CONNECT and the /api/connect endpoint validate before committing an edge?
How is data-ui-ref consumed by the surrounding UI resolver, and what distinguishes run:// from ui:// addresses?
Why is NODE_STATES not made into a tldraw signal or React store to avoid direct reads?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's stated purpose is a pure extraction of the generic node shape, edges overlay, and graph loader with no behavior change; the content matches that, adding registry-driven status/error handling and backend-owned layout.
apparent_themes: Backend as source of truth for graph layout and edge semantics.
Registry-driven UI vocabulary for node states and port schemas.
Fail-loud status surfacing, especially for failed and stuck nodes.
Canvas as a reflecting view rather than an owning editor.
Single generic shape type replacing per-node-type implementations.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to make the visual canvas a thin, generic renderer whose node appearance, status vocabulary, and layout are all served from the backend or a registry, while keeping connection logic and shape synchronization explicit and local.
novelty: It is unusual for a tldraw-based canvas to derive status colors, labels, and dot shapes entirely from a served state registry, and to implement port-to-port wiring with setPointerCapture plus elementFromPoint rather than tldraw's built-in arrow shapes.
contribution: It contributes a reusable generic node shape, a shared port-positioning helper, backend-sync graph loading and refresh primitives, and a screen-space edge overlay that a fused system could keep as the canvas rendering layer.
summary_for_embedding: A tldraw-based generic node shape and graph synchronization module that renders registry-driven node cards with per-port nubs, status and error surfacing, pointer-drag connections, and an SVG edge overlay.
intention_for_embedding: To extract the canvas node rendering and graph-loading behavior into one file where node appearance and layout are driven by backend state and schema registries.

symbols.does: Declares the tldraw base shape type for generic nodes.
symbols.description: Defines the NodeShape TLBaseShape with props such as dimensions, nodeId, nodeType, kind, status, output, address, ref, layer, and error so tldraw can serialize the shape and the component can render node state.

symbols.code_id: code://company/canvas/app/src/NodeShape.tsx::shapeIdFor | function | export const shapeIdFor = (nodeId: string) => createShapeId('n-' + nodeId)
symbols.does: Maps a backend node id to a stable tldraw shape id.
symbols.description: Prefixes the node id with 'n-' and calls createShapeId so loadGraph, refresh, and Edges can consistently reference the same tldraw shape for a given backend node.

symbols.code_id: code://company/canvas/app/src/NodeShape.tsx::portTop | function | export function portTop(i: number, n: number) {
symbols.does: Computes the vertical position of a port within a node card.
symbols.description: Spreads n ports evenly across NODE_H so the i-th port aligns with both the rendered nub CSS top and the edge overlay's page-space y coordinate.

symbols.does: Registers and renders the generic 'node' tldraw shape.
symbols.description: Defines the shape type, default props, geometry, resize/rotate behavior, and React component that draws the node card, status dot, output/error body, per-port nubs, and connection gestures.

symbols.code_id: code://company/canvas/app/src/NodeShape.tsx::nub | function | const nub = (side: 'in' | 'out', port: string, i: number, n: number) => {
symbols.does: Renders an input or output port nub and wires connection gestures.
symbols.description: Positions a port label using portTop; input nubs carry data attributes for drop detection, while output nubs capture pointer events and resolve the drop target via elementFromPoint before committing a connection.

symbols.code_id: code://company/canvas/app/src/NodeShape.tsx::loadGraph | function | export async function loadGraph(editor: Editor) {
symbols.does: Synchronizes tldraw shapes with the backend graph, creating, updating, or pruning nodes.
symbols.description: Fetches the graph via api.graph, maps each backend node to a tldraw shape using backend-provided positions, updates existing shapes, creates missing ones, deletes orphaned shapes, and fits the camera only on an initially empty page.

symbols.does: Updates existing tldraw node shapes with the latest backend state.
symbols.description: Applies status, output, error, layer, and optional position updates to shapes that already exist, without creating or pruning shapes.

symbols.code_id: code://company/canvas/app/src/NodeShape.tsx::portY | function | const portY = (nodeType: string, side: 'inputs' | 'outputs', port?: string) => {
symbols.does: Looks up the page-space y offset of a named port on a node.
symbols.description: Reads the registered port list for the node's type from getOINFO, finds the port's index, and returns the same portTop value used by the card nubs so edges land precisely on their nubs.
